query: in Interactive complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Customer complaints: Top 7 common complaints & how to resolve ...
results.content:
#### Complicated interactive voice response (IVR) system

Customers can quickly become frustrated if they can’t access the right department with the choices offered on the interactive voice response (IVR) system or have no option to talk to a live customer support agent. A survey conducted by CSG found that 36% of respondents would rather wait on hold to speak with a human agent than use an AI-powered virtual assistant to resolve their issue. [...] Staff confusion

When customers reach out for support, they become flustered if they reach a call center agent who can only provide scripted responses that don’t resolve their problem. They’re also frustrated if they’ve previously talked to another customer support agent, their new rep doesn’t have the context of the situation, and they need to start from scratch. Make sure that your customer support tools can gather the context of your customer’s problem in advance so you know exactly where to route him for help. [...] One way to solve the problem of how to connect customers with accurate information quickly is to implement a self-service solution that they can use to search for relevant content in your company’s knowledge base. Also, give customers a way to connect with a rep in the right department if they can’t find the answers they need on their own.

### 3. Customers have trouble navigating the automated system

Getting stuck in phone menu limbo was a serious issue for customers. Make sure the customer experiences  your service team provides don’t include a loop that customers can’t escape due to a:

#### Complicated interactive voice response (IVR) system

results.title: Interactive Brokers is rated "Average" with 3.3 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Jun 21, 2026

Image 53: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## I decided to try Interactive Brokers…
I decided to try Interactive Brokers after hearing so many recommendations from experienced investors.

The signup process was one of the worst, most frustrating onboarding experiences I've ever had.

In about 90 minutes I encountered:

• A maintenance window with no information or ETA. The only reason I discovered it was because I'm a software engineer who is used to checking status pages when something isn't working. Most users would have no idea what was happening.

• Misleading logout errors instead of maintenance messages.

• Inconsistent .ie and .co.uk signup flows.

• Compliance questions asking for details most people simply don't know.

• Security questions that feel like they're from 2005. [...] Evaluating 1,628 reviews, most reviewers were somewhat happy with their experience overall. Many customers appreciate the platform's professional-level tools, low fees, and extensive range of trading options, including access to global exchanges. Reviewers also frequently highlight the efficiency and helpfulness of the customer service when they manage to connect with a representative. The ability to trade for extended hours is seen as a significant advantage by many users. However, some customers also noted significant frustration with the website and user interface, describing it as complicated, slow, and prone to glitches. There are also frequent complaints about the difficulty in reaching customer support and the lengthy response times for inquiries. Some users found the process of [...] 1/5 stars.

Will update this review if the actual post-signup experience turns out to be significantly better than the onboarding process.

June 21, 2026

Unprompted review

Useful

Share

results.title: Interactive Brokers (UK) Limited - Complaints Handling Policy and Procedures Summary | Interactive Brokers LLC
results.content:
If more than eight weeks from the date of your complaint have past and you haven’t received a final response from us, or you are dissatisfied with the final response you have received from us, you are entitled to refer your complaint to the Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS), they can be contacted at the following:

  

 By post, address:  

  The Financial Ombudsman Service  
   Exchange Tower  
   London  
   E14 9SR
 By telephone:  

  Tel within the UK: 0800 023 4567 or 0300 123 9123  
   Tel when calling from abroad: +44(0)2079640500
 By Email:  
  complaint.info@financial-ombudsman.org.uk
 Online:  
   

You have the right to refer your complaint to the Financial Ombudsman Service, free of charge – but you must do so within six (6) months of the date of our Final Response to you. [...] Investigate each complaint competently, diligently and impartially;
 Assess each complaint fairly, consistently and promptly with respect to subject matter, whether it should be upheld and what remedial action or redress may be appropriate;
 Offer redress or remedial action when appropriate;
 Explain to the complainant promptly and, in a way that is fair, clear and not misleading, its assessment of the complaint, its decision on it and any offer of remedial action or redress; and
 Comply promptly with any offer of remedial action or redress accepted by the complainant.
 Consider complaints as an opportunity to learn, adapt, improve and provide our customers a better service;
 Increase customer confidence in our management commitment to treat them fairly. [...] In case we are unable to complete our investigation and resolve your complaint within four weeks, we will keep you updated on the progress of your complaint and communicate with you in accordance with the FCA's rules on dispute resolution.

We then will endeavour to send a final response to you within eight weeks of receipt of your complaint. If we are unable to provide you with a final response within this time frame, we will write to you explaining why we are unable to issue a final response at this time and advise you when you can expect a final response.

Once our investigation has been completed, you will be sent a final response with our findings and the outcome of your complaint.
